title: task
description:
router.py:596 - The _haversine function uses math.asin(math.sqrt(a)) where a is calculated as the sum of squared sine terms. Due to floating-point precision errors, a can exceed 1.0 by a tiny amount for near-antipodal points. When sqrt(a) > 1.0, math.asin() raises ValueError('expected a number in range from -1 up to 1'). This crashes GPSR forwarding.

Failure scenario: Two nodes at near-antipodal positions (e.g., (0,0) and (0,179.9999...)) where floating-point errors push a slightly above 1.0.

Fix: Clamp the argument to asin: c = 2 * math.asin(min(1.0, math.sqrt(a)))
close_reason: Fixed: Clamped sqrt(a) to max 1.0 in haversine asin() call to prevent ValueError from floating-point errors on near-antipodal points
